openmeter/ingest/kafkaingest.purpose: Implements ingest.Collector by producing CloudEvents to per-namespace Kafka topics via confluent-kafka-go. Owns per-call topic resolution/provisioning, OTel span lifecycle per event, the fixed Kafka header set, and the KafkaProducerGroup oklog/run pair for async delivery reporting. Its child sub-packages split by concern: serializer/ (key/value encoding) and topicresolver/ (namespace→topic mapping).

patterns.name: Serializer injection for key and value
patterns.description: Collector never encodes events itself — it delegates to serializer.Serializer inside every Ingest call: SerializeKey encodes a dedupe.Item (namespace+source+id) for sink dedup, SerializeValue encodes a flat CloudEventsKafkaPayload.
patterns.example: key, _ := s.Serializer.SerializeKey(topicName, namespace, ev); value, _ := s.Serializer.SerializeValue(topicName, ev)

patterns.name: Topic resolved + provisioned per Ingest call
patterns.description: TopicResolver.Resolve maps namespace→topic; TopicProvisioner.Provision (idempotent, no-op on existing) ensures the topic exists before Produce. Both run per call, not at construction.
patterns.example: topicName, _ := s.TopicResolver.Resolve(ctx, namespace); _ = s.TopicProvisioner.Provision(ctx, pkgkafka.TopicConfig{Name: topicName, Partitions: s.TopicPartitions})

patterns.name: OTel span wraps the full Ingest with deferred error recording
patterns.description: Ingest opens a span at entry and a deferred closure captures the local 'err' variable, calling span.RecordError + span.SetStatus on any non-nil error. Do not shadow 'err' with := inside the body.
patterns.example: ctx, span := s.Tracer.Start(ctx, "openmeter.ingest.process.event", ...); defer func() { if err != nil { span.RecordError(err); span.SetStatus(otelcodes.Error, err.Error()) }; span.End() }()

patterns.name: Fixed Kafka header set per message
patterns.description: Every kafka.Message carries four headers: 'namespace' ([]byte), 'specversion', 'ingested_at' (RFC3339Nano UTC via ToIngestedAt), and otelx.OTelSpanContextKey. The sink worker reads these to reconstruct ctx and attribute tenancy.
patterns.example: Headers: []kafka.Header{{Key: HeaderKeyNamespace, Value: []byte(namespace)}, {Key: "specversion", Value: []byte(ev.SpecVersion())}, {Key: HeaderKeyIngestedAt, Value: []byte(ToIngestedAt(clock.Now()))}, {Key: otelx.OTelSpanContextKey, Value: spanCtx}}

patterns.name: KafkaProducerGroup as oklog/run pair
patterns.description: KafkaProducerGroup returns (execute, interrupt) for the caller's run.Group; execute drains producer.Events() handling *kafka.Message delivery reports, *kafka.Stats, and kafka.Error. The caller must start it — Produce alone drops delivery results.
patterns.example: execute, interrupt := kafkaingest.KafkaProducerGroup(ctx, producer, logger, kafkaMetrics); runGroup.Add(execute, interrupt)

patterns.name: NamespaceHandler reuses Resolver + Provisioner
patterns.description: NamespaceHandler.CreateNamespace calls Resolve then Provision; DeleteNamespace is a no-op unless DeletionEnabled=true. The TopicResolver nil-check is deferred to call time, so a zero-value handler panics in production.
patterns.example: namespaceManager.RegisterHandler(kafkaingest.NamespaceHandler{TopicResolver: resolver, TopicProvisioner: provisioner, Partitions: 4, DeletionEnabled: false})

key_file_guides.role: Collector struct (NewCollector validates non-nil deps), Ingest produce path, Close, and KafkaProducerGroup; ToIngestedAt/FromIngestedAt header time helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Producer.Produce is fire-and-forget; without KafkaProducerGroup running, delivery failures are silent. Do not shadow the deferred-captured 'err'.

key_file_guides.role: NamespaceHandler implementing namespace.Handler for ingest topics; provisions on create, deprovisions only when DeletionEnabled=true.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Zero-value handler panics at call time (deferred nil-check). Register before initNamespace() in cmd/server/main.go or the default namespace misses provisioning.

key_file_guides.role: JSONSerializer: SerializeKey (dedupe.Item→key bytes) and SerializeValue (CloudEventsKafkaPayload→value bytes); wire format consumed by the sink worker's FromKafkaPayloadToCloudEvents.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CloudEventsKafkaPayload.Time is unix int64 (timezone intentionally dropped); Data is a JSON string, not raw bytes. Changes must be mirrored in the sink deserializer.

key_file_guides.role: Serializer interface with GetKeySchemaId/GetValueSchemaId hooks; the JSON impl returns 0 for both.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A schema-registry-aware serializer must return real IDs from all four methods or the sink worker mis-deserializes.

key_file_guides.role: NamespacedTopicResolver maps namespace→topic via fmt.Sprintf(template, namespace); default 'om_%s_events'.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Constructor validates non-empty template but not %s count — zero or two placeholders silently produce wrong topic names.

key_file_guides.role: Resolver interface, ctx-accepting for future async/DB-backed implementations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always propagate the caller's ctx to Resolve — never context.Background() in application code.

anti_patterns: Calling Producer.Produce without running KafkaProducerGroup in an oklog/run.Group — delivery errors are silently dropped.
Hard-coding topic names instead of calling TopicResolver.Resolve per namespace.
Adding new Kafka headers in Ingest without updating the sink worker's header parsing.
Constructing Collector/NamespaceHandler with a nil TopicResolver — the nil-check is deferred and panics in production.
Storing timezone-aware time in CloudEventsKafkaPayload.Time — it is unix int64 with timezone intentionally stripped.

decisions.decision: Topic is resolved and provisioned on every Ingest call, not only at namespace creation.
decisions.rationale: Guarantees idempotent topic existence even when NamespaceHandler was not called first (self-hosted single-namespace), at the cost of one idempotent admin check per call.

decisions.decision: Key encodes dedupe.Item (namespace+source+id), not the event ID alone.
decisions.rationale: Partitions by logical event identity so the sink worker's Redis dedup hash matches the Kafka key with no extra lookup, preventing double-counting on consumer restart.

decisions.decision: KafkaProducerGroup is an oklog/run pair returned to the caller, not a goroutine in NewCollector.
decisions.rationale: Lets cmd/* control delivery-reporting lifecycle via the shared run.Group for graceful shutdown and no goroutine leaks, consistent with all cmd/* entrypoints.

code_examples.scenario: Wiring Collector and registering NamespaceHandler in app/common
